sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Napoli are in strong form, sitting at the top of the Serie A standings with 18 points and a recent 3-1 win over Inter. Lecce, however, are struggling in 16th place with only 6 points, managing just one win in their last five matches, a narrow 1-0 victory against Parma.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Napoli have dominated recent encounters with Lecce, winning four of the last five matchups, including the last two head-to-head games (1-0 each). Lecce have failed to score in their last four matches against Napoli, highlighting their difficulty in breaking through Napoli’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Napoli boast an impressive attacking output with 15 goals scored this season, while Lecce have struggled offensively with just 7 goals and rank poorly in big chances created (20th in the league). Defensively, Lecce have conceded 13 goals, one of the highest in the league, compared to Napoli’s tighter backline conceding only 8.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Napoli’s ability to score in key periods (especially between 46-60 minutes) and their high final third entries (averaging 62 per game in recent matches) suggest they will control possession and create chances. Lecce’s reliance on late goals (3 in the 76-90 minute period) may not be enough against a team of Napoli’s quality.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Napoli’s superior form, head-to-head dominance, and statistical edge, they are heavily favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 69% probability for an away victory. Lecce’s defensive vulnerabilities and lack of attacking threat make an upset unlikely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, the scoreline remains goalless between Lecce and Napoli at the Via del Mare. Despite the lack of goals, the first half was far from dull, with both teams showing moments of intent. Napoli dominated much of the play, controlling possession and creating more threatening opportunities. Lecce, however, held firm defensively and managed to carve out a few attacking moments of their own. The game remains finely poised as both sides head into the second half with everything to play for.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Lecce
halftimeSections.text: Lecce struggled to assert themselves in the first half, particularly in terms of ball retention and creating clear-cut chances. Their defensive structure was tested often, but they managed to keep Napoli at bay with some resolute defending. Players like Tiago Gabriel stood out with crucial contributions at the back. Going forward, they showed limited penetration in the final third, with minimal entries into dangerous areas. They will need to be more adventurous in the second half if they are to break the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Napoli
halftimeSections.text: Napoli were the more dominant side in the opening 45 minutes, enjoying the lion's share of possession and generating more opportunities in attack. They entered the final third frequently and showed intent in the penalty area, though they failed to convert their chances, missing a significant opportunity. Defensively, they looked solid, winning a high percentage of duels and aerial battles. Players like Noa Lang and Juan Jesus were notable for their contributions. Napoli will likely push harder in the second half to capitalize on their superiority.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Napoli, who started strongly with consistent attacking pressure in the opening stages. They maintained a series of dangerous spells, particularly in the middle of the half, where they looked likely to score. Lecce had a brief resurgence with a notable spell of attacking intent around the midway point, putting some pressure on Napoli's defense. However, Napoli regained control towards the end of the half with several sustained periods of threat, keeping Lecce pinned back. This pattern suggests Napoli are more likely to dictate the tempo after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Lecce, Lameck Banda showed flashes of creativity and could be pivotal in unlocking Napoli's defense if given more space in the second half. Medon Berisha also poses a potential threat up front and will look to make an impact. On Napoli's side, Mathias Olivera was a standout with his involvement in attack, despite missing a key chance. Billy Gilmour's ability to create opportunities in midfield could be crucial in breaking down Lecce's backline. Both teams have individuals capable of changing the game in an instant.
